words.example: Better internet can accelerate online learning.

words.meaning: to make something wider or more general
words.example: Reading newspapers can broaden your view of society.

words.meaning: to stop happening or stop doing something
words.example: The factory will cease production next month.

words.meaning: to change something into a different form
words.example: The city will convert an old station into a library.

words.meaning: to make a small official change
words.example: Lawmakers plan to amend the traffic rule.

words.meaning: to join two or more things together
words.example: Two local hospitals will merge next spring.

words.meaning: to agree to do a task or responsibility
words.example: The lab will undertake a study of water quality.

words.meaning: to experience a change or process
words.example: The airport will undergo repairs this summer.

words.meaning: to force a rule, tax, or duty on people
words.example: The government may impose a fine for illegal parking.

words.meaning: to be greater than a number or limit
words.example: The cost should not exceed the approved budget.
